user_stories.goal: Add items from multiple stalls into one cart and checkout once
user_stories.benefit: Pay one time with accurate totals and fees
acceptance_criteria: Cart groups items by stall and shows per-stall subtotals plus overall total
Checkout processes a single payment and creates one platform order

user_stories.goal: Split a paid order into per-stall sub-orders and sync to each stall system
user_stories.benefit: Merchants can fulfill independently without losing allocation accuracy
acceptance_criteria: Sub-orders contain only that stall’s items with itemized taxes/fees allocations
Each sub-order is created in the corresponding stall system via connector/API

user_stories.goal: Retry failed split/sync steps safely with full auditability
user_stories.benefit: Reduce manual fixes and prevent duplicate sub-orders
acceptance_criteria: Split/sync operations are idempotent using a stable order/sub-order key
Admin can view failure reason and trigger retry without creating duplicates

user_stories.goal: View and update only my stall’s sub-orders in real time
user_stories.benefit: Fulfill faster with clear scope and minimal tooling
acceptance_criteria: Merchant portal/API lists only sub-orders for the authenticated stall
Status updates (e.g., confirmed/shipped) persist and propagate to the platform order

user_stories.goal: Track one consolidated order with per-stall fulfillment visibility
user_stories.benefit: Understand what is happening without contacting support
acceptance_criteria: Order page shows overall status plus per-stall sub-order statuses
Updates reflect latest merchant changes within a bounded delay

user_stories.goal: Auto-generate a versioned flowchart of the order-splitting logic on deploy/CI
user_stories.benefit: Keep documentation current and reduce onboarding/debug time
acceptance_criteria: CI generates and stores an SVG/PNG flowchart tied to the release version
Flowchart reflects current split steps and error/retry paths
